description_long: This is a vintage-style corded phone with a rectangular, boxy base made of black material, featuring a separate handset connected by a coiled cord. The front panel is equipped with multiple rotary knobs, switches, and indicator lights, suggesting it is a specialized or industrial communication device, possibly for use in control rooms or security posts. The handset rests on top of the base, and there is a visible cradle area for placing the receiver. The surface of the base is slightly worn, with some smudges and a green label or sticker on the top. The coiled cord is thick and robust, indicating durability for frequent use. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function over aesthetics, and the materials appear to be primarily hard plastic and metal.
description: A black, boxy corded phone with a coiled cord, multiple front-panel knobs, and a worn, utilitarian appearance. The handset rests on top, and the device looks built for industrial or specialized use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the handset resting on top of the rectangular base, with the coiled cord partially visible and a green label on the upper surface.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the control panel with multiple knobs, switches, and indicator lights, as well as the coiled cord connecting the handset to the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the handset lying on the base and the coiled cord extending outward, green label visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back and top of the base, with the handset resting on the cradle and minimal detail on the back panel.
